creator: ian@niw.com.au
text:
I cannot provide the exact svn revision, but I think it was around 14th of July 11.2 stable that it changed. I have an application that runs a slave sub process chrooted and with capsicum restrictions. It updates its status using setproctitle periodically while in the capsicum state. Prior to updating the os over the weekend, the setproctitle was working as expected. Following the upgrade the setproctitle call is failing to change the process status, if I disable the capsicum operations, the setproctitle works as expected.

11.2 r335757M: Thu Jun 28 14:57:57 CST 2018 works as expected, setproctitle
for capsicum process updates normally

ntest 244   0.0  0.3 27684 23904  -  SC   23:08   0:00.02 ntest: Throughput: Vars=0 Files=0 Size=0 Tests=0 Complete=0 TestZones=0

11.2 r337748M: Wed Aug 15 10:31:35 CST 2018 rebuilt today for the same binary shows

ntest  971   0.0  0.3 39088 24792  0  SC+  22:07      0:06.79 ntest -R Probe

Which is the arguments it was started with, rather than its current status.

Not sure if this is related, but it seems coincidental timing wise
------------------------------------------------------------------------
r336449 | kib | 2018-07-18 19:24:32 +0930 (Wed, 18 Jul 2018) | 3 lines

MFC r335939, r336088:
Add setproctitle_fast(3) for frequent callers.
